hero.headline: Designing with intention, growing through craft.
hero.intro: I'm Jane — a product designer shaping thoughtful experiences at Intuit. What started in a family business became freelance work, hackathons, community leadership, and a practice grounded in systems, accessibility, and impact.

cta.label: Begin My Story
cta.hint: Jump to where it all started — then scroll up toward the present.

milestones.description: I collaborate with designers and engineers to improve reusable components, accessibility, documentation, and workflows.
milestones.learned: Designing at scale taught me how design systems create consistency across products.

milestones.description: Worked on branding, websites, and visual design for clients while learning agency workflows and professional collaboration.
milestones.learned: Studio work sharpened how I present ideas, iterate with clients, and ship polished visual design.

milestones.description: Designed for nonprofits alongside cross-functional teams and learned how UX can create meaningful social impact.
milestones.learned: Impactful design balances empathy, constraints, and clarity for the people who need it most.

milestones.description: Lead Canada's largest student-run hackathon across design, development, logistics, sponsorship, marketing, and operations.
milestones.learned: Leading at scale means designing experiences for teams, not just screens.

milestones.description: Discovered product design through hackathons and learned rapid prototyping and collaboration.
milestones.learned: Building under pressure taught me to move from idea to interface with speed and intention.

milestones.description: Transitioned from graphic design into UX, accessibility, interaction design, and product thinking.
milestones.learned: Formal training grounded my practice in research, craft, and designing for real people.

milestones.description: Started a club to introduce students to digital design through workshops and creative projects.
milestones.learned: Building community taught me that design culture is something you create, not just consume.

milestones.description: Worked with small businesses on branding, presentations, and graphic design.
milestones.learned: Client work early on built my eye for detail and confidence shipping real deliverables.

milestones.description: My design journey began helping my family's business with social media, websites, and promotional materials, which sparked my passion for design.
milestones.learned: Hands-on work showed me that design is a tool for connection — and I've been chasing that ever since.

next.headline: See the work behind the journey.
next.description: Explore case studies, side projects, and the experiments that shaped how I design today.
